title: Cheese Sandwich
description: A classic, simple cheese sandwich. Yes, this counts as cooking.
steps: Butter both slices of bread.
Layer cheese slices on one side.
Close the sandwich. Congratulations, chef.

title: Ham Sandwich
description: A hearty ham sandwich. Slightly fancier than cheese. Barely.
steps: Butter both slices of bread.
Layer ham slices on one side.
Close the sandwich. Try not to brag about your culinary skills.

title: Hagelslag Sandwich
description: A Dutch classic — chocolate sprinkles on bread. Don't knock it till you've tried it.

ingredients.name: Hagelslag
ingredients.subtitle: Milk chocolate, dark chocolate, fruit-flavored, etc.

steps: Butter a slice of bread generously.
Cover the buttered side with a thick layer of hagelslag.
Optionally top with a second slice, fold it closed, or eat open-faced like the maniac you are.

title: Other Sandwich
description: We don't have what you want, so figure it out yourself.

ingredients.name: Bread
ingredients.subtitle: White, whole wheat, sourdough, rye, ciabatta, etc.

ingredients.name: Butter or spread
ingredients.subtitle: Butter, mayo, mustard, hummus, etc. To taste

ingredients.name: Filling of choice
ingredients.subtitle: Peanut butter, jam, Nutella, cream cheese, cold cuts, veggies, etc.

steps: Spread your condiment of choice on the bread.
Add your filling.
Close the sandwich. You're on your own from here.

title: Tosti
description: A hot, crispy grilled sandwich. The pinnacle of Dutch engineering.
steps: Butter the outside of both slices of bread.
Layer cheese (and any extras) between the slices.

options: Tosti iron: close and grill until golden, about 3–4 minutes.
Frying pan: toast on medium heat, flipping once, until golden on both sides.
Oven/grill: place on a baking sheet at 200°C until the cheese melts and bread is crispy.

steps: Cut diagonally. This is non-negotiable.

title: Fried Egg
description: A simple egg. Somehow people still manage to mess this up.
steps: Heat a pan on medium with a little butter or oil.

options: Sunny side up: crack into the pan, cook without flipping until the white is set but the yolk is runny.
Over easy: cook sunny side up, then flip and cook for just 15–30 seconds.
Over medium: flip and cook for about 1 minute for a partially set yolk.
Over hard: flip and cook for 2–3 minutes until the yolk is fully set.
Scrambled: whisk eggs in a bowl, pour into the pan, and stir gently until just set.
Omelette: whisk eggs, pour into the pan, let set, add fillings, and fold in half.

steps: Season with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately. Cold eggs are a cry for help.

title: I'm Bringing My Own
description: Too good for our recipes? Fine. Here's how to heat your leftovers.

ingredients.name: Your food
ingredients.subtitle: Leftovers, meal prep, takeout, anything goes

steps: Bring your food in a sealed container.
Store in the fridge if it needs to stay cool.

options: Microwave: transfer to a microwave-safe container, heat in 1-minute intervals, stir in between.
Oven: preheat to 180°C, place in an oven-safe dish, and heat for 10–15 minutes.
Stovetop: pour into a pan and warm on medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Eat it cold: some things are just fine at room temperature.

steps: Enjoy your meal. You're welcome for the microwave instructions.
